rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/spec-driven-workflow.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Use the local app-dev spec workflow
rules.requirement: This workspace uses a translated local spec-driven workflow inspired by spec-kit and does not require specify-cli, a vendored .specify tree, or upstream workflow runtime support.
evidence_required: Workflow changes use local app-dev standards, commands, scripts, and templates.
No dependency on specify-cli, upstream .specify runtime files, or vendored workflow runtime is introduced without a recorded exception.

rules.title: Keep principles in constitution and mechanics in delegated standards
rules.requirement: standards/constitution.md is the stable principle set. Keep operational mechanics in this workflow standard and other delegated standards instead of duplicating them in the constitution.
evidence_required: Principle changes are kept in standards/constitution.md.
Operational workflow mechanics are kept in standards/spec-driven-workflow.md or delegated standards.

rules.title: Keep app AGENTS as durable app identity and active spec pointer
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/AGENTS.md owns durable app identity, stack constraints, verification rules, and the active spec pointer.
evidence_required: App AGENTS.md contains current app identity, stack constraints, verification rules, and active spec pointer.
Active spec pointer is updated before material implementation begins.

rules.title: Keep spec intent in spec.md
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/specs/NNN-<slug>/spec.md owns feature intent, requirements, acceptance criteria, data impact, and risk.
evidence_required: Feature intent, requirements, acceptance criteria, data impact, and risk are captured in spec.md.
Plan and implementation remain traceable to the active spec.

rules.title: Keep architecture rollout and verification decisions in plan.md
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/specs/NNN-<slug>/plan.md owns architecture, rollout, verification, and implementation constraints derived from that spec.
evidence_required: Plan.md records architecture, rollout, verification, and implementation constraints derived from the active spec.
Plan.md is current before material implementation begins.

rules.title: Keep implementation checklist in tasks.md
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/specs/NNN-<slug>/tasks.md owns the ordered implementation checklist for that feature.
evidence_required: Tasks.md contains an ordered implementation checklist for the active feature.
Completed and deferred task state is accurate before handoff.

rules.title: Keep workflow evidence in workflow-receipts.md
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/specs/NNN-<slug>/workflow-receipts.md owns workflow classification, Applicable Standards Checklist evidence, and closure evidence for UI, data, mobile, and release-readiness obligations.
evidence_required: workflow-receipts.md records applicable UI, data, mobile, and release-readiness workflow obligations.
workflow-receipts.md records the Applicable Standards Checklist when implementation standards selection is in scope.
Closure evidence is populated before completion is claimed.

rules.title: Use checklist.md for gated sensitive work
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/specs/NNN-<slug>/checklist.md owns the gated review checklist for sensitive work.
evidence_required: Gated or sensitive work includes a populated checklist.md in the active spec folder.
Checklist content is created no earlier than the tasks phase when required by the gated path.

rules.title: Use the spec workflow plan template for per-spec plans
rules.requirement: templates/spec-workflow/plan.template.md is the reusable per-spec plan shape for generated and updated plans.
evidence_required: Generated or refreshed per-spec plans use templates/spec-workflow/plan.template.md as the plan shape.
Template drift is validated when plan template ownership changes.

rules.title: Start generated apps with specs 001 initial
rules.requirement: Start every generated app with specs/001-initial/.
evidence_required: Generated apps include projects/<app>/specs/001-initial/.
Initial spec artifacts pass current-template validation for generated apps.

rules.title: Use monotonically increasing zero-padded spec numbers
rules.requirement: Later feature work uses specs/00N-<feature-slug>/, with numbers zero-padded to three digits and increasing monotonically.
evidence_required: New feature specs use specs/00N-<feature-slug>/ format.
Spec numbers are zero-padded to three digits and increase monotonically.

rules.title: Point AGENTS and active plan to active spec before implementation
rules.requirement: AGENTS.md and the active spec's plan.md must both point to the active spec path before material implementation begins.
evidence_required: projects/<app>/AGENTS.md points to the active spec path.
The active spec plan.md points to the same active spec path before material implementation begins.

rules.title: Use compatibility mode for established apps
rules.requirement: compatibility is the default validation mode for existing apps under projects/<app>/ and enforces the durable minimum contract without retroactively failing older apps because app-dev later densified a template, command, or validator.
evidence_required: Ordinary work on established apps uses compatibility validation unless a migration is explicitly in scope.
Validator changes do not retroactively fail older app artifacts solely due to template densification.

rules.title: Use current-template mode only for generated or refreshed artifacts
rules.requirement: current-template is the source-of-truth contract for newly generated apps, disposable scaffold verification, and apps explicitly refreshed to the current template shape.
evidence_required: Newly generated, disposable scaffold, or explicitly refreshed apps use current-template validation.
current-template validation is not applied to established apps unless an opt-in migration is recorded.

rules.title: Keep phase execution in command files
rules.requirement: The standards document owns the phase narrative. Command files own exact execution steps, working directory, and receipt obligations.
evidence_required: Phase semantics are updated in standards/spec-driven-workflow.md.
Exact execution steps, working directories, and receipt obligations are updated in .agents/commands/<phase>.md.

rules.title: Map every workflow phase to its command and enforcement
rules.requirement: Clarify, Spec, Plan, Tasks, Analyze, Implement, Converge, Verify, and Release readiness phases each have a command owner and main enforcement surface.
evidence_required: Every workflow phase has an owning .agents/commands/<phase>.md file or documented gated branch.
Main enforcement scripts remain aligned with the phase map.

rules.title: Use lean path for ordinary app and module work
rules.requirement: Use spec -> plan -> tasks -> analyze -> implement -> converge -> verify -> handoff for ordinary app and module work.
evidence_required: Ordinary app and module work follows the lean path unless gated triggers apply.
Handoff identifies the command path used.

rules.title: Require lean path artifacts, registry selection, and checks
rules.requirement: Lean-path work requires a complete spec.md, current plan.md, tracked tasks.md, current workflow-receipts.md, analyze-spec before material implementation, Analyze-phase visibility into likely implementation-time registry pressure when triggered, applicable registry rules resolved during implementation, converge before verify so remaining work returns to implement when needed, check-spec-artifacts before final verification handoff, validate-workflow-receipts with verification evidence before completion, and verify-app plus rendered UI checks before completion.
evidence_required: Lean-path required artifacts exist and are current.
Analyze reports likely critical or high implementation-time rule pressure before /implement when relevant registry families are triggered.
Applicable registry rules are resolved and recorded during implementation.
Converge closes the done or not-done decision before /verify.
analyze-spec, check-spec-artifacts, validate-workflow-receipts -RequireVerificationEvidence, and verify-app results are recorded before completion.
Rendered UI checks are recorded when UI is in scope.

rules.title: Use gated path for sensitive work
rules.requirement: Use clarify -> spec -> checklist -> plan -> tasks -> analyze -> implement -> converge -> verify -> handoff for work involving auth, payments, secrets, public APIs, data access, file uploads, RLS, AI tool actions, deployment, or live migrations.
evidence_required: Sensitive work follows the gated path.
The triggering sensitive surface is identified in the active spec, plan, checklist, or receipt.

rules.title: Require gated checklist and closure evidence
rules.requirement: Gated-path work requires a populated checklist.md created no earlier than /tasks, resolved analyze-spec contradictions before material implementation, Analyze-phase visibility into likely implementation-time registry pressure when relevant, converge before verify so remaining implementation work returns to /implement, populated workflow-receipts.md with relevant sections closed, explicit security, data, and rollback notes, and explicit user approval before destructive or live-environment operations.
evidence_required: Gated checklist is populated no earlier than /tasks.
Analyze-spec contradictions are resolved before material implementation.
Analyze may preflight likely implementation-time registry blockers without taking ownership of implementation evidence.
Converge closes the post-implementation done or not-done decision before /verify.
Security, data, rollback, and approval evidence is recorded for relevant gated surfaces.

rules.title: Close convergence through artifact reconciliation
rules.requirement: Convergence is artifact-based. Before claiming a feature is complete, reconcile implemented scope, plan architecture and verification decisions, task state, workflow receipts, governed docs, and handoff notes.
evidence_required: Implemented scope still matches active spec.md.
plan.md, tasks.md, workflow-receipts.md, touched governed docs, and handoff notes reflect the implemented state before completion.

rules.title: Use converge template for explicit convergence notes
rules.requirement: Use templates/spec-workflow/converge.template.md as the default handoff structure when a feature needs an explicit convergence note.
evidence_required: Explicit convergence notes use the converge template structure unless a specific reason is recorded.

rules.title: Verify only after convergence is closed
rules.requirement: In command terms, /converge closes the post-implementation done or not-done decision first, and /verify then records verification evidence against that converged state.
evidence_required: Converge closes remaining implementation work before verification evidence is recorded.
Verify command assumes a converged state and does not re-own remaining-work discovery.
